properties.remark: PUBLIC REPORTED BRIEF WATERSPOUT OVER NORTHERN SARASOTA BAY.

*** 3 INJ *** EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AS WELL AS STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES. LOCATED IN SW CAPE CORAL NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CHIQUITA BLVD S AND BEACH PKWY W TO NEAR MOHWAK PKWY AND SKYLINE BLVD. TIME APPROXIMATELY BETWEEN 2345 AND 2356 UTC. ADDITIONAL PICTURES WERE RECEIVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF MULTIPLE CARS FLIPPED OVER AND SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED INCLUDING ONE WITH A WALL COLLAPSE. THREE MINOR INJURIES WERE REPORTED BY LEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AS WELL AS STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES. LOCATED IN SW CAPE CORAL NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CHIQUITA BLVD S AND BEACH PKWY W TO NEAR MOHWAK PKWY AND SKYLINE BLVD. TIME APPROXIMATELY BETWEEN 2345 AND 2356 UTC.

BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED REPORTS AND PICTURES OF A METAL ROOF RIPPED OFF A BUILDING IN VENICE AND LANDED ON CARS IN THE PARKING LOTS. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED A REPORT AND A PICTURE OF A ROOF PARTIALLY BLOWN OFF A CONDO ON FEATHER SOUND DRIVE IN THE WHISKEY CREEK AREA AS WELL AS DAMAGE TO PLAM TREES. THE PICTURE SHOWED WIDELY SCATTERED ROOFING MATERIAL AND SCORING ALONG THE TRUNK OF A TREE. ADDITIONAL UNCONFIRMED REPORTS OF A TORNADO TOUCH DOWN WERE RELAYED BY LEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT. TIME WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MULTIPLE MEDIA REPORTS FROM THE BROADCAST MEDIA AND GENERAL PUBLIC OF TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES DOWN IN GATEWAY ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS.
ASOS STATION SOUTHWEST FLORIDA INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT /RSW/ MEASURED A 60 MPH/52 KNOT THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.

NWS AND LEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT STORM SURVEY CREW FOUND AROUND 6 HOUSES IN LEHIGH ACRES WITH STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE FROM FRIDAYS THUNDERSTORMS. DAMAGE INCLUDED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE...FACIA AND PEICES OF LANAI ENCLOSURES RIPPED FROM HOUSES...AND SNAPPED TREES. BASED OFF OF THE DEGREE OF DAMAGE...WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT UP TO 80 MPH. ALL OF THE DEBRIS WAS BLOWN IN A UNIFORM DIRECTION...INDICATING STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE LIKELY TO BLAME.
A PICTURE OF LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN IN LEHIGH ACRES WAS POSTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

NWS AND LEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT STORM SURVEY CREWS FOUND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE FROM FRIDAYS THUNDERSTORMS IN LEHIGH ACRES ON SHELDON AVENUE JUST SOUTH OF E 6TH STREET. A COUPLE OF LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED...ONE HOUSE HAD A FEW SHINGLES BLOWN OFF...AND A METAL SHED WAS BLOWN OVER. ALL OF THE DEBRIS WAS BLOWN IN A UNIFORM DIRECTION...INDICATING THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE LIKELY TO BLAME.
